title: Methods & `this`
description: Functions attached to objects — and the this trap.

questions.explanation: Method call: `this` is the object before the dot.
Detached call: `this` is undefined in strict mode → TypeError reading .name.
call sets `this` explicitly. bind/apply do similar things.
Arrows have LEXICAL this — they don't get one from the caller.
bind returns a NEW function permanently bound to `this`.
Borrowing a method with call — same as arr.slice(1).

questions.code:
function greet(g) { return g + ', ' + this.name; }
console.log(greet.call({ name: 'Ada' }, 'Hi'));
questions.explanation: call takes this first, then args. apply takes args as an array.
